key_points: A tissue is a group of similar cells that perform a specific function
Multicellular organisms have tissues that help in division of labour
Plant tissues are mainly of two types: meristematic tissues and permanent tissues
Meristematic tissues are responsible for plant growth
Permanent tissues are formed from meristematic tissues after differentiation
Animal tissues are classified into epithelial, connective, muscular and nervous tissues
Each type of tissue performs specific functions in the body
examples: Meristematic tissue example: Apical meristem at root tips
Complex permanent tissue example: Xylem and phloem
formulas: Xylem function → Transport of water and minerals
Neuron parts → Cell body + Dendrites + Axon
Types of muscle → Striated + Smooth + Cardiac
exam_tips: Remember the difference between meristematic and permanent tissues
Learn the functions of parenchyma, collenchyma and sclerenchyma
Xylem transports water while phloem transports food
Animal tissues include epithelial, connective, muscular and nervous tissues
Practice diagrams of neuron and plant tissues for exams
